questions.text: The difference between the highest and lowest observations in a data set is called:
questions.explanation: Range = Highest Value - Lowest Value.

questions.text: The arithmetic mean of 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 is:
The observation that occurs most frequently in a data set is called:
questions.explanation: Mode is the most common value.

questions.text: Find the range of the following data: 2, 10, 5, 8, 20, 15
questions.explanation: Highest (20) - Lowest (2) = 18.

questions.text: The value which lies in the middle of a data set when arranged in order is:
questions.explanation: Median is the middle value.

questions.text: Find the median of the data: 24, 36, 46, 17, 18, 25, 35
questions.explanation: Arranged: 17, 18, 24, 25, 35, 36, 46. The 4th value is 25.

questions.text: Probability of a sure event is:
questions.explanation: A sure event always happens, so its probability is 1.

questions.text: Probability of an impossible event is:
questions.explanation: Impossible events never happen, so probability is 0.

questions.text: When a coin is tossed, what is the probability of getting a Head?
questions.explanation: Total outcomes = 2 (H, T). Favorable = 1 (H). Prob = 1/2.

questions.text: A die is thrown. What is the probability of getting a number 7?
questions.explanation: A standard die only has numbers 1 to 6.

questions.text: The mean of first five natural numbers is:
The mean of first five whole numbers is:
In a bar graph, the bars have ______ width and ______ gaps between them.
questions.explanation: Standard properties of a bar graph.

questions.text: What is the mode of the data: 1, 1, 2, 4, 3, 2, 1, 2, 2, 4?
questions.explanation: 2 appears 4 times, 1 appears 3 times. Mode is 2.

questions.text: Probability of drawing a Red ball from a bag containing 3 Red and 2 Blue balls is:
questions.explanation: Favorable (3) / Total (5) = 3/5.

questions.text: Mean is also known as:
questions.explanation: Arithmetic mean is the common average.

questions.text: The data represented by points joined by line segments is a:
questions.explanation: Definition of a line graph.

questions.text: Range of the data 35, 50, 48, 62, 27, 39, 43 is:
If mean of 4, 6, x, 9, 10 is 7, then x is:
Which measure is best for 'most popular' items?
questions.explanation: Mode shows frequency, indicating popularity.

questions.text: Tally marks are usually grouped in sets of:
questions.explanation: Four vertical lines and one diagonal across them.

questions.text: Probability of getting an even number on a die is:
questions.explanation: Even numbers: 2, 4, 6 (3 outcomes). 3/6 = 1/2.

questions.text: Double bar graphs are useful for:
questions.explanation: Used for comparison, e.g., marks in two different terms.

questions.text: The sum of all probabilities of a trial is:
questions.explanation: Total probability of all outcomes always sums to 1.

questions.text: Range of first 10 prime numbers is:
Can mode be more than one for a data set?
questions.explanation: Data sets can be bimodal or multimodal.

questions.text: Mean is always one of the numbers in a data set.
questions.explanation: Mean can be a fraction or a value not present in the set.

questions.text: Median is always one of the numbers in a data set (if count is odd).
questions.explanation: If count is odd, median is the middle observation.

questions.text: A coin is tossed 100 times. Heads appeared 45 times. Frequency of Tails is:
Probability of drawing a king from a deck of 52 cards is:
questions.explanation: There are 4 kings in a deck. 4/52 simplifies to 1/13.

questions.text: Numerical information is called:
questions.explanation: Basic definition of data.

questions.text: Finding 'central' values is called measures of:
questions.explanation: Mean, median, mode are measures of central tendency.

questions.text: Mode of 2, 2, 3, 3, 4, 4 is:
questions.explanation: All three values have the same maximum frequency.

questions.text: Probability always lies between:
questions.explanation: Probability is never negative and never greater than 1.

questions.text: The 'average' height of students in a class is calculated by:
questions.explanation: Mean provides the standard average height.

questions.text: Representing data using pictures is:
questions.explanation: Definition of pictograph.

questions.text: Median of 10, 15, 20, 25, 30 is:
questions.explanation: Data is already ordered. Middle value is 20.

questions.text: Mode of a set of data is always one of the numbers in the data.
questions.explanation: Mode must be a value that exists in the observations.

questions.text: Data Handling involves:
questions.explanation: It covers the entire lifecycle of data analysis.
